title: myrsine modesta subsp. coriaria
intro: Myrsine modesta subsp. coriaria, also known as Coriaria, is a plant species within the family Coriariaceae, characterized by its distinctive foliage and small, red berries. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of coriariin and coriariin derivatives. The health benefits associated with Coriaria include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of Coriaria typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves or berries, which can be used to treat various health conditions, though more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
